Cratylia oroboides, commonly known as 'Cracca', is a legume native to South America, used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Cratylia oroboides include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cratylia oroboides include teas, infusions, and tinctures, often used to treat gastrointestinal disorders, fever, and skin irritations.
